title: Annabel Lee by Edgar Allan Poe — Love Poetry Lines
content:
It was many and many a year ago,
  In a kingdom by the sea,
That a maiden there lived whom you may know
  By the name of ANNABEL LEE;
And this maiden she lived with no other thought
  Than to love and be loved by me.

_I_ was a child and _she_ was a child,
meaning: "Annabel Lee" by Edgar Allan Poe is a love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 46 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "It was many and many a year ago,   In a kingdom by the sea,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edgar Allan Poe's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
